title: Eta-5 interceptor

{{Quote|Looks fast.|Wedge Antilles comments on Rhysati Ynr's choice of craft at Kessel|<ref name="FJ1">''[[Fate of the Jedi: Outcast]]''</ref>}}
The '''Eta-5 interceptor''' was a type of [[Interceptor/Legends|interceptor]] in use by the [[New Jedi Order]] and the [[Galactic Alliance Defense Fleet|Galactic Alliance Navy]] as of [[40 ABY/Legends|40 ABY]]. Developed by [[Kuat Drive Yards/Legends|Kuat Drive Yards]], it was a descendant of the [[Eta-2 Actis-class interceptor/Legends|Eta-2 interceptor]], but it was also regarded as a type of [[A-wing]].

==Characteristics==
{{Quote|Get ready to do some shooting, Eight.|Syal Antilles to a wingmate during Operation Roundabout|<ref name="LF1" />}}
Built by [[Kuat Drive Yards/Legends|Kuat Drive Yards]],<ref name="CSWE" /> the Eta-5 possessed a pair of wing arrays similar to its [[Eta-2 Actis-class interceptor/Legends|Eta-2]] predecessor, but had an overall hull shape in line with [[A-wing]]s, with [[Solar collector/Legends|solar panels]] mounted on struts extending from midway up the fuselage.<ref name="FJ1" /> The [[Interceptor/Legends|interceptor]] had a cramped [[Cockpit/Legends|cockpit]] equipped with rocker arms on the seat, and was powered by [[Twin ion engine/Legends|twin ion engines]], giving it top-of-the-line speed and maneuverability, but it had light [[Armor/Legends|armor]] and minimal [[Deflector shield/Legends|shielding]]. It was equipped with dual fire-linked [[Concussion missile/Legends|concussion missile]] launchers, twin [[Laser-cannon energizer|laser cannons]], and advanced [[Computer/Legends|computer]]-aided [[Targeting computer/Legends|targeting]] systems, giving the Eta-5 considerable punching power when it needed to engage small [[Capital ship/Legends|capital ships]].<ref name="LF1" /> Due to its wedge-shaped hull, the Eta-5 was referred to as an [[A-wing]] by some pilots.<ref name="LF7">''[[Legacy of the Force: Fury]]''</ref>

==History==
{{Quote|Is there a problem with my interceptor?|Syal Antilles to an undercover Corellian agent|<ref name="LF1" />}}
In [[40 ABY/Legends|40 ABY]],<ref name="TEA">''[[The Essential Atlas]]''</ref> [[Wedge Antilles/Legends|Wedge Antilles's]] daughter, [[Syal Antilles]], flew this model when she entered the [[Galactic Federation of Free Alliances|Galactic Alliance]] [[Galactic Alliance Defense Force|military]] under the name of Lysa Dunter. She joined [[VibroSword Squadron]] based out of the [[Galactic-class battle carrier|''Galactic''-class battle carrier]] [[Dodonna (Galactic-class)|''Dodonna'']] and flew as [[VibroSword Seven]] during [[Operation Roundabout]] at [[Corellia/Legends|Corellia]] before the onset of the [[Second Galactic Civil War]]. However, she was forced to pull out of the [[Squadron/Legends|squadron]] due to illicit [[Corellian/Legends|Corellian]] activities, and was reassigned to serve as a [[Pilot/Legends|pilot]] for the new [[Aleph-class starfighter|''Aleph''-class starfighters]].<ref name="LF1" />

Pilot [[Rhysati Ynr]] made use of an Eta-5 during [[Lando Calrissian/Legends|Lando Calrissian's]] mission to [[Mission to save Kessel|prevent]] the [[Mundicide|destruction]] of [[Kessel/Legends|Kessel]], and loaned it briefly to Wedge Antilles when he expressed an interest in flying it. Wedge later used the fighter to protect the [[Millennium Falcon/Legends|''Millennium Falcon'']] from an [[energy spider]].<ref name="FJ1" />

==Behind the scenes==
The Eta-5 interceptor made its first appearance in the [[2006]] novel ''[[Legacy of the Force: Betrayal]]''.<ref name="LF1" /> In the [[2007]] novel ''[[Star Wars: Legacy of the Force]]:'' ''[[Legacy of the Force: Fury]]'', Syal Antilles reflects that her combat experience has been limited to "A-wings and Alephs". As she had seen battle in Eta-5s and Alephs in the earlier novel ''Legacy of the Force: Betrayal'', this references the fact that the Eta-5 was in fact, in part, a derivative of the earlier [[RZ-1 A-wing interceptor/Legends|RZ-1 A-wing interceptor]] from [[Incom Corporation/Legends|Incom Corporation]] as well as being a descendant of the Eta-2 interceptor.<ref name="LF7" /> This implication is continued in detail in the [[2009]] [[Star Wars: Fate of the Jedi|''Fate of the Jedi'']] novel ''[[Fate of the Jedi: Outcast]]'', in which the Eta-5 is described as appearing similarly to an A-wing fighter, with strut-mounted [[Solar collector/Legends|solar panels]] protruding from midway up the fuselage.<ref name="FJ1" />

''[[The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia]]'' mentions that the Eta-5 was also utilized by the [[New Jedi Order]], but no source has ever depicted this.<ref name="CSWE" />
